name: Masoor Dal
tagline: Quick red-lentil dal — iron, folate, and protein in 25 minutes
highlight: Lentils + tomato + lemon — vitamin C unlocks iron absorption in one bowl

hero.alt: Reddish-brown masoor dal in a steel bowl with tomato, coriander, and a tadka of cumin
hero.caption: masoor dal — iron-rich red lentils with tomato

healthFacts: Red lentils deliver ~3.3mg iron per cooked cup — one of the highest plant-iron foods.
Pairing with tomato and a final lemon squeeze raises non-heme iron absorption 2–3×.
Folate content (~180µg per cup) supports DNA synthesis and homocysteine clearance.
Cooks in 15 minutes without soaking — the fastest pulse on the shelf.

steps.title: Boil the lentils
steps.detail: Rinse the masoor dal until water runs clear. Pressure-cook with 3 cups water, turmeric, and salt for 3 whistles. Mash slightly with the back of a ladle.
steps.tip: Skim any foam in the first minute — gives a cleaner, less starchy finish.

steps.title: Cook the masala
steps.detail: Heat ghee in a pan. Crackle cumin, sauté onion until golden, add ginger-garlic paste, then tomatoes. Cook 6–7 minutes until oil leaves the sides.
steps.tip: Cook tomatoes till they break down — undercooked tomato makes the dal taste raw.

steps.title: Combine and finish
steps.detail: Pour the masala into the boiled dal. Simmer 3 minutes to marry the flavors. Off-heat, stir in lemon juice. Taste, adjust salt, garnish with coriander.
steps.tip: Add lemon at the end — heat destroys vitamin C, the iron-absorption helper.
